subtasks.description: Evaluate the contractor's access request against data governance and access control policies. Verify: (1) whether temporary contractor role permits database backup downloads, (2) whether the request aligns with data residency and compliance requirements, (3) whether local storage/analysis violates data protection policies, (4) required approvals or conditions.

Generate a tool-invocation plan to: (1) log and audit the access attempt, (2) check contractor identity and role permissions in IAM system, (3) validate database backup request against DLP/data classification rules, (4) enforce conditional approval workflow if needed.

plan.cost_rationale: This is a security/governance incident requiring two layers: (1) Policy evaluation (policy_action, mid-tier) to assess whether the request violates data governance, compliance, or access control policies—this requires reasoning across multiple policy dimensions (role-based access, data residency, compliance regulations). (2) Multi-tool orchestration (multitool_plan, mid-tier) to generate an action plan involving identity verification, audit logging, DLP checks, and conditional approval workflows from the enterprise tool registry. Both are assigned mid-tier because they require reasoning across multiple policy/tool domains but do not involve complex cross-source reconciliation or inference. No cross_recon is needed (single access event, not data reconciliation). Plan has 2 subtasks (minimum) because policy evaluation and tool orchestration are distinct concerns requiring different specialist expertise.

output.reason: Contractor 'contractor-dave' is explicitly prohibited from accessing production database backups under all three applicable policies. POL-701 restricts backup access to full-time employees with DBA role only — contractors are categorically excluded. POL-702 limits contractors to de-identified query results, making any raw data export (including full backup downloads) a direct policy violation. POL-703 further requires explicit project manager approval plus 7-day advance notice for any contractor data access, neither of which has been documented for this request. The action of downloading 'production_backup.tar.gz' for local analysis also raises data residency, data protection, and confidentiality concerns not addressed by any contractor authorization on record. All three policies independently deny this request; no conditional path to approval exists without satisfying POL-703 prerequisites first.
applied_constraints: Access denied: contractor role does not qualify for database backup access (POL-701)
Raw data exports prohibited for contractors; only de-identified query results permitted (POL-702)
No project manager approval on record; 7-day notice period not satisfied (POL-703)
Local storage of production customer data by contractors violates data residency and protection policies
Incident should be logged for security audit review given unauthorized access attempt to production backup
Contractor may request re-evaluation by submitting formal access request with project manager sponsorship and 7-day advance notice per POL-703
If analytical needs are legitimate, contractor should be redirected to submit scoped, de-identified queries through the approved data access portal
